relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Balaam prepares seven altars beneath a king's demand.
Balak and Balaam offer upon each altar.
Balaam leaves the king standing while he seeks a word.
Divine meets the hired seer among his arranged sacrifices.
YHWH places an unpurchasable word in Balaam's mouth.
He returns to the waiting king and princes.
The oracle begins by naming the commissioned curse.
Balaam cannot curse what El has not cursed.
From the heights he sees a people with a distinct calling.
He cannot count Jacob's abundance and longs for an upright end.
Balak discovers that his purchased curse has become blessing.
Balaam remains bound to the word placed in his mouth.
The king changes the viewpoint, hoping partial sight will change truth.
Another height and another seven altars are prepared.
Again Balaam leaves Balak beside the offering.
Again YHWH gives the word.
Balak waits to hear whether power has finally bent blessing.
The second oracle commands the king to listen.
Divine promise is not royal propaganda that shifts with convenience.
The blessing received cannot be reversed for payment.
The oracle sees covenant presence rather than a purchasable accusation.
The Liberator from Egypt is strength for the people.
No technique of enchantment can override what El is doing.
Ancient lion imagery speaks of a people rising in power.
Balak would now prefer silence to uncontrollable blessing.
Balaam repeats the boundary: only YHWH's word.
The king tries a third location.
He brings Balaam to Peor overlooking wasteland.
Seven more altars are requested.
The king repeats the ritual, still unable to purchase the outcome.

contemplative_note: Numbers 23 is about the failure of manipulation. Balak changes payment, prestige, altars, and viewpoint, yet blessing remains outside royal control. Distinct identity here is not superiority; it is freedom from being defined by an enemy's purchased curse.
